negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing scheduled subpool insertion/removal accounting rule: no scheduled pool path exists that avoids incrementing calldata counters on insertion and therefore avoids decrementing them on removal/prune.
related_but_insufficient_code: The port has no crates/transaction-pool/src/pool/scheduled.rs and no add_to_scheduled_pool path.
Pool mod only detects SCHEDULED_TX_TYPE_ID to avoid async sender-lock rejection; it then inserts into the regular TxPool path.

notes: Confirmed gap under the strict audit rule; absence of scheduled pool accounting is not treated as not-applicable.
rationale: Scheduled transactions enter through add_to_scheduled_pool and never increment normal calldata counters, so scheduled removal paths must not decrement those counters.
statement: At current tip, removing or pruning a scheduled transaction from the scheduled pool must not decrement total_calldata_bytes or per-sender calldata counters, because scheduled transactions did not increment those counters on insertion.
suggested_falsification_tests: Add normal transactions to consume calldata counters, add a scheduled transaction through add_to_scheduled_pool, remove or prune the scheduled transaction by hash, and assert total_calldata_bytes and sender_calldata are unchanged by the scheduled removal.
summary: Scheduled removal calldata-counter behavior is absent with the old scheduled subpool.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Add normal calldata-counted transactions plus a scheduled transaction through a scheduled subpool and remove the scheduled hash; current port has no scheduled subpool/counter path to satisfy the assertion.
title: Scheduled removals do not decrement calldata counters
